title: centaurea × belgradensis
intro: Centaurea × belgradensis, also known as Balkan cornflower, is a hybrid plant species within the daisy family, derived from the cross between Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea virgata. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Consuming Centaurea × belgradensis is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of allergies, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
